description: Add scrollbar change markers to diff viewer — colored indicators on the scrollbar showing where additions and deletions are in the file.

userStories.title: Scrollbar Change Markers for Inline Diff View
userStories.description: As a user, I want colored markers on the scrollbar showing where changes are in the diff so I can quickly navigate to them.
acceptanceCriteria: A thin overlay column (8-10px wide) is rendered on the right edge of the inline diff view, overlaying or beside the scrollbar
The column spans the full height of the scrollable area
For each changed line in the diff, a small colored marker is rendered at the proportional vertical position: green for additions, red for deletions
Marker height is proportional — a single changed line is 2-3px, a hunk of 10 changed lines is ~15-20px
Markers are semi-transparent so overlapping ones blend (opacity 0.6-0.7)
Clicking a marker scrolls the diff to that position
The marker column has position: sticky or absolute so it stays visible while scrolling horizontally
Works for both inline diff view and the full diff view
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implementation: render a thin absolute-positioned div on the right edge of the scroll container (.inlineView or .fullDiffPane). Calculate each changed line's position as (lineIndex / totalLines) * containerHeight. Render small colored divs at those positions. For click-to-scroll: onClick calculates the target scrollTop from the click position ratio and scrolls the container. The diff data (hunks with line types) already exists — just need to extract line positions.

userStories.title: Scrollbar Change Markers for Side-by-Side Diff View
userStories.description: As a user, I want change markers on both panes of the side-by-side diff view.
acceptanceCriteria: Each pane (left/old and right/new) in the side-by-side view gets its own marker column
Left pane markers: red for deleted lines at their proportional positions
Right pane markers: green for added lines at their proportional positions
Context lines (unchanged) have no markers
Clicking a marker scrolls that pane to the position (and the other pane syncs via existing scroll sync)
Markers work in both the regular side-by-side diff view and the full diff view
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Same approach as US-SM-001 but applied to each .sbsPane. The SideBySideDiffView already has scroll sync — clicking a marker on one pane should trigger the scroll handler which syncs the other pane. Each pane's marker column is independently positioned.

userStories.title: Viewport Indicator on Scrollbar Markers
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see where my current viewport is relative to the change markers.
acceptanceCriteria: A semi-transparent rectangle overlay on the marker column shows the currently visible portion of the file
The rectangle's position and height are proportional to scrollTop and clientHeight relative to scrollHeight
The rectangle updates in real time as the user scrolls
The rectangle has a subtle border (1px var(--border)) and very light background (rgba white 5%)
The viewport indicator is behind the change markers (lower z-index) so markers are always visible
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Listen to the scroll event on the diff container. Calculate viewport rect: top = (scrollTop / scrollHeight) * markerColumnHeight, height = (clientHeight / scrollHeight) * markerColumnHeight. Render as an absolute-positioned div. Update via onScroll handler with requestAnimationFrame for smooth updates.
